title: Text Inspection, Search, and Structured Transformation · Mastery Check
summary: Extract a defensible incident summary from mixed logs without silently corrupting delimiters, encodings, ordering, or duplicate policy. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select cat, less, head, tail, wc, file, and od from file size, format, encoding, and inspection goal.
Use fixed strings, basic or extended regular expressions, anchors, classes, context, and exit status with explicit locale assumptions.
Separate find traversal, predicates, grouping, pruning, printing, and actions while protecting path boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Text Inspection · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Logs contain duplicate IDs, spaces, UTF-8 names, and severities high, low, and HIGH. Produce a stable 4-row summary with explicit locale, delimiter, regex, sort key, and duplicate policy.

Which action demonstrates Text Inspection without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select cat, less, head, tail, wc, file, and od from file size, format, encoding, and inspection goal.

content.value: The Open Group Base Specifications Issue 8 — Controls portable shell, utility, process, file, environment, and system-interface semantics.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: cat is the safest viewer for every binary or unbounded file.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Field Transform Tools: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Logs contain duplicate IDs, spaces, UTF-8 names, and severities high, low, and HIGH. Produce a stable 6-row summary with explicit locale, delimiter, regex, sort key, and duplicate policy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose cut, paste, tr, sed, awk, or a structured parser based on delimiter, record, escaping, and mutation requirements.
